heading: Definitions
label: 1732 Definitions
notes: Amendments2008—Pars. (3) to (9). added par. (3) and redesignated former pars. (3) to (8) as (4) to (9), respectively.Pub. L. 110–2461996—Par. (6). added par. (6) and struck out heading and text of former par. (6). Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “The term ‘indigenous nongovernmental organization’ means an organization that operates under the laws of the recipient country, or that has its principal place of activity in such country, and that works at the local level to solve development problems in the foreign country in which it is located, except that the term does not include an organization that is primarily an agent or instrumentality of the foreign government.”Pub. L. 104–1271990— amended section generally, substituting present provisions for provisions defining agricultural commodity.Pub. L. 101–6241984— inserted “(including fish, without regard to whether such fish are harvested in aquacultural operations)” and struck out provision that subject to the availability of appropriations therefor, any domestically produced fishery product could be made available under this chapter.Pub. L. 98–6231981— substituted “wine, beer, distilled spirits, or other alcoholic beverage” for “wine or beer”.Pub. L. 97–981979— inserted reference to beer.Pub. L. 96–721977— required that in allocation of funds made available under subchapter II of this chapter priority be given to financing the sale of food and fiber commodities.Pub. L. 95–1131971— inserted sentence that proviso excluding alcoholic beverages from term “agricultural commodity” shall not be construed as prohibiting domestic wine industry from participating in market development activities for expansion of export sales of domestic agricultural commodities.Pub. L. 92–421966— substituted definition of agricultural commodity and provisions as to availability of fishery products for former provisions respecting agreements for delivery of surplus agricultural commodities, period, and security for payments in relation to long-term supply contracts, now covered by subchapter II of this chapter. See .Pub. L. 89–808section 1707 of this title1962— authorized executive agreements with financial institutions acting in behalf of friendly nations and administrative sales agreements with foreign and United States private trade with provision for security for payments.Pub. L. 87–703

children.heading: Administrator
children.label: 1 Administrator
children.text: The term “Administrator” means the Administrator of the Agency for International Development, unless otherwise specified in this chapter.

children.heading: Agricultural commodity
children.label: 2 Agricultural commodity
children.text: The term “agricultural commodity”, unless otherwise provided for in this chapter, includes any agricultural commodity or the products thereof produced in the United States, including wood and processed wood products, fish, and livestock as well as value-added, fortified, or high-value agricultural products. Effective beginning on , for purposes of subchapter III, a product of an agricultural commodity shall not be considered to be produced in the United States if it contains any ingredient that is not produced in the United States, if that ingredient is produced and is commercially available in the United States at fair and reasonable prices.October 1, 1991

children.label: A
children.text: the Committee on Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry of the Senate;

children.label: B
children.text: the Committee on Agriculture of the House of Representatives; and

children.label: C
children.text: the Committee on Foreign Affairs of the House of Representatives.

children.heading: Cooperative
children.label: 4 Cooperative
children.text: The term “cooperative” means a private sector organization whose members own and control the organization and share in its services and its profits and that provides business services and outreach in cooperative development for its membership.

children.heading: Developing country
children.label: 5 Developing country
children.text: The term “developing country” means a country that has a shortage of foreign exchange earnings and has difficulty meeting all of its food needs through commercial channels.

children.heading: Food security
children.label: 6 Food security
children.text: The term “food security” means access by all people at all times to sufficient food and nutrition for a healthy and productive life.

children.heading: Nongovernmental organization
children.label: 7 Nongovernmental organization
children.text: The term “nongovernmental organization” means an organization that works at the local level to solve development problems in a foreign country in which the organization is located, except that the term does not include an organization that is primarily an agency or instrumentality of the government of the foreign country.

children.heading: Private voluntary organization
children.label: 8 Private voluntary organization
children.text: The term “private voluntary organization” means a not-for-profit, nongovernmental organization (in the case of a United States organization, an organization that is exempt from Federal income taxes under ) that receives funds from private sources, voluntary contributions of money, staff time, or in-kind support from the public, and that is engaged in or is planning to engage in voluntary, charitable, or development assistance activities (other than religious activities).section 501(c)(3) of title 26

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 9 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of Agriculture, unless otherwise specified in this chapter.
